meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop hunting for ideas. Learn a high-leverage client workflow to build profitable SaaS tools using Antigravity and Claude Code. Advanced guide for founders.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start mining for 'pain.' By implementing a client-centered workflow—interviewing service-based businesses to uncover manual spreadsheet bottlenecks—you create guaranteed product-market fit. Use Antigravity to architect the logic and Claude Code to execute the build, turning operational friction into a recurring revenue SaaS product.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks scrolling through forums and trend reports, looking for the 'next big idea,' yet you remain paralyzed by the fear of building something nobody wants. This isn't a lack of creativity; it's a lack of market validation. 

True founders don't invent problems; they observe existing, expensive, and manual workflows. By shifting your focus from 'what to build' to 'what is currently broken for paying clients,' you bypass the validation stage entirely. Today, we bridge the gap between messy client operations and scalable software using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Identify the Spreadsheet Bottleneck
steps.description: Find a local business or service provider currently managing a critical process in Excel or Airtable. Your goal is to identify a repetitive task that costs them more than 5 hours per week.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business process description: [Insert Process]. Identify the top 3 manual bottlenecks that could be automated via a specialized web app. Provide a feature list for a lean MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Interview a service provider about their manual spreadsheet tasks and suggest an MVP feature set.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Transform the manual process into a relational data structure. This ensures your MVP isn't just a UI wrapper, but a functional engine that handles their actual data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Supabase-compatible schema for a [Type of App] that manages [Specific Process]. Include tables for clients, tasks, and status tracking with strict RLS policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create migrations for the database schema generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Generation
steps.description: Use your defined schema to build the dashboard. Focus on high-density data views rather than fancy animations; your users value efficiency over aesthetic fluff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React/Tailwind dashboard component that displays the [Table Name] data. Include a CRUD interface for modifying entries and a search filter.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the frontend components for the dashboard based on the schema.'

steps.title: Implement Business Logic
steps.description: Connect the frontend to your backend logic. This is where the 'magic' happens—automating the actual work the client used to do manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic to trigger a [Specific Action, e.g., Email/Report] whenever a row status in the [Table Name] is updated to 'Complete'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Implement the server-side business logic for status-based automation.'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate with Feedback
steps.description: Push to production via Vercel or your preferred provider. Present the live link to your client immediately to capture 'real-world' friction points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for this application, including environment variable management and monitoring setups.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Deploy the application to Vercel and configure production environment variables.'

pro_tips: Always charge a setup fee for the MVP; it separates the 'curious' clients from the 'serious' ones.
Build the 'Export to CSV' feature first; it gives your client an exit strategy and builds massive trust.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your data transformation functions to ensure no manual data is corrupted.
Limit your MVP scope to exactly one user role; role-based access control is a feature-creep trap.
If the client doesn't use the tool within 48 hours of delivery, the problem you identified wasn't actually painful enough.
content.next_steps: Once your first client is using the tool, record a screen-share tutorial, package it as a subscription service, and reach out to three similar businesses in the same niche.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing what the market wants and start building what your clients are begging for. Scale your expertise, automate the manual, and own your niche. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
